question.content: Nitrogen at 25 C and 100 kPa is reversibly adiabatically compressed to 15000 kPa. Compute the final temperature using the provided real-gas model relations. Output exactly one integer, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no percent sign, no fraction, no equation form, and no extra text. Answer format: a single integer; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Apply entropy-balance equation for reversible adiabatic compression with the given Cp(T) and state equation form.
step_1.key_point: Entropy-balance equation for reversible adiabatic compression with the given Cp(T).

step_3.content: Analyze panel 1 of 2 and record the intermediate relation needed before moving to the next panel.
step_3.key_point: Set up governing relation for the final temperature using the provided real-gas model relations.

step_5.content: Solve the resulting nonlinear equation for final temperature.
step_5.key_point: The resulting nonlinear equation for final temperature.
